mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market and robust economic expansion, which typically translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment typically supports consumer spending.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is consistently positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in retail sales suggests strengthening consumer demand, which boosts corporate revenues and profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth exceeds 5% annualized for 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is also low, it signals an accommodative monetary environment that supports economic growth and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Low rates and low inflation typically support equity markets.
failure_condition: If both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are below their respective thresholds, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate during an economic contraction signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which negatively impacts consumer confidence and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Rising unemployment often precedes or accompanies recessions.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months during a contraction, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production indicates a robust manufacturing sector and overall economic health, which supports corporate profitability and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key coincident economic indicator.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows positive month-over-month growth, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts indicates a weakening housing market and broader economic slowdown, which can dampen consumer spending and investment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average decline > 10% from prior 3-month average
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market health is a leading indicator for economic activity.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 10% on a 3-month average basis, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and unemployment (UNRATE) is low, it signals an overheating economy, likely prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which negatively impacts equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation and low unemployment often lead to Fed tightening.
failure_condition: If both CPIAUCSL is above 3.0% and UNRATE is below 5.0%, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong GDP growth, corporate earnings are expected to be robust, driving investor confidence and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a primary driver of corporate profits.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 3.0% during an expansion, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances.
